question: Can an Ascend-like compact partial emit plus detached reduction improve the real-LI compressed dKV many-to-one accumulation wall?

implementation.mechanism: Precompute per-route-tile compact slot metadata, emit one compact partial row per active compressed group and D tile, then reduce compact rows into dKV with index_add.
diagnostic_limitations: Still uses materialized probs and grad_logits_scaled inputs.
Uses a PyTorch index_add reducer after compact partial emission.
Tests the compact-row accumulation schedule, not the final fused sparse MLA/LI/compressor backward.

decision.read: The compact format is correct and cuts dense partial rows nearly in half at S4096, but the per-route-tile local accumulation and emit schedule is still slower than the retained grouped atomic path. The detached reducer itself is small; the wall is producing the compact partials, not the final index_add.
decision.closed_branch: compact rows with per-route-tile LDS/local atomics plus index_add reducer
decision.next_branch: Avoid per-contribution local atomic accumulation. Try warp/block segmented reduction or group-owned MFMA accumulation over route-ordered tiles, then fuse target_probs, LI derivative, compressor recompute, dQ, and dK/dV into the same sparse attention backward pipeline.
